evals.prompt: Where should I post my new app? It's a budgeting tool for couples who keep separate bank accounts. I'm active on Reddit personally but never posted about my product anywhere. Scared of getting banned.
evals.expected_output: A community launch kit: 5-8 named communities (specific subreddits like r/personalfinance, r/relationships-adjacent money subs, Discords, forums), a spread-out posting schedule, 3 custom post drafts tailored per community, response protocol, and per-community ban risks.
assertions: At least 5 specific named communities are recommended with relevance to couples/personal finance
Post drafts are uniquely written for different communities, not one draft reused
The drafts lead with the problem/story rather than 'check out my app'
Ban-risk warnings or community rules are addressed per platform
A posting schedule spreads submissions over multiple days rather than all at once

evals.prompt: i posted my app in 6 subreddits yesterday, same text in all of them. two got removed and one mod muted me. how do i fix this and keep launching on reddit?
evals.expected_output: A candid diagnosis (copy-paste cross-posting is the exact behavior spam filters and mods target), damage-control steps (don't repost soon, engage genuinely, possibly apologize to the mod), and a corrected relaunch plan with per-community unique posts.
assertions: The output identifies identical cross-posting as the cause of removals
Damage-control steps are given before any new posting
The relaunch plan requires uniquely-written posts per community
The output includes contribute-before-you-take guidance (genuine engagement before promotion)
